codeReview.summary: GeneratorAgent implements ReAct loop with think→act→observe→repeat cycle, but tool_call_results in AgentResult are never populated when a tool registry is configured. The test only exercises the fallback path (no LLM or tools), not the actual ReAct loop path.

issues.description: When tool_registry is Some, execute() runs execute_step_with_react but never populates tool_call_results. The ReactLoopSummary.steps contain the actions (via ReactStep.action), but these are not extracted and returned in AgentResult.tool_calls. The field remains empty Vec::new().
total_tokens is always 0u64 even when ReAct loop executes with LLM. The LLM.chat call in decide_action_with_llm returns token usage but it is not captured or propagated.

The test test_generator_agent (line 3932) only tests the MVP fallback path where no LLM or tool_registry is configured. It does not exercise the ReAct loop path with actual tool execution. The test passes but doesn't verify the core functionality described in the feature.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The commit included fixes to pre-existing compile errors in swell-tools (fs module collision, Vec type annotation, pattern type). These were not part of the feature scope but were necessary for the build to pass. This is acceptable but worth noting.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: git show ccf2fe0 shows changes to crates/swell-tools/src/tools.rs that fix compile errors not related to GeneratorAgent

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-worker skill file was not found at .factory/skills/rust-worker/SKILL.md, so I could not verify if the worker followed the expected procedure or if there were skillFeedback deviations.

summary: GeneratorAgent's ReAct loop is properly implemented with think→act→observe→repeat cycle and tool integration via ToolRegistry. However, there is a blocking bug where tool_call_results and total_tokens in AgentResult are never populated even when the ReAct loop executes. The test only covers the fallback path, not the actual ReAct implementation. The implementation satisfies VAL-AGENTS-002 (execute returns success=true) but does not fully meet the feature description of 'uses tools to read/write files, produce code changes' since the tool calls are not tracked in the result.
